generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryArmActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromechanical actuators driving rotational movement of the gantry arm via Arduino-based microcontrollers for low-level signal processing"
]

instance CartActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromechanical actuators controlling linear movement of the gantry carriage via Arduino-based microcontrollers"
]

instance PuckCarrierActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromechanical actuators managing horizontal movement of container pucks via Arduino-based controllers"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Swing Angle Encoder measuring rotational position of the gantry arm for feedback on crane orientation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionData
    DTDFVocab:producedData VelocityData
    DTDFVocab:producedData AccelerationData
    DTDFVocab:producedData LoadMeasurement
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Anemometer measuring environmental conditions (temperature/humidity) relevant to motion dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityData
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance KinematicStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance LoadMeasurementData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance PositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Position data derived from Swing Angle Encoder"
    DTDFVocab:fromData KinematicStateData
]

instance VelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Velocity data derived from Swing Angle Encoder"
    DTDFVocab:fromData KinematicStateData
]

instance AccelerationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Acceleration data derived from Swing Angle Encoder"
    DTDFVocab:fromData KinematicStateData
]

instance LoadMeasurement : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Load measurement data derived from Swing Angle Encoder"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LoadMeasurementData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GantryCADModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric CAD model of the gantry crane structure (overhead beam, moving carriage, supporting frame) derived from Autodesk Fusion360"
]

instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical kinematic model implementing inverse/forward kinematics for predicting crane motion dynamics"
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model governing optimal trajectory computation for container movements based on operational constraints"
]

instance SimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Customized simulation environment implementing the kinematic model for real-time validation of trajectories"
]

instance TimescaleDBDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing high-frequency operational data (crane position logs, motor currents) for real-time and historical analysis"
]

instance RealTimeData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current operational metrics (position, velocity, acceleration, load measurements) transmitted in real-time via MQTT"
]

instance ProcessedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data processed through Continuous Validation Service for alignment between physical and digital states"
]

instance ValidationMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Validation metrics comparing simulated vs. real trajectories (e.g., residual swinging motion tolerance)"
]

instance OperationalConstraintsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data representing operational constraints (payload capacity, speed limits, positional accuracy) for trajectory optimization"
]

// C11: Enablers
instance Fusion360CADTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Autodesk Fusion360 used for creating parametric CAD models of the gantry crane structure"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GantryCADModel
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker facilitating lightweight, low-latency data streaming between physical sensors and digital services"
]

instance TimescaleDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for storing and querying high-frequency operational data (position logs, sensor readings)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDataStore
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance GrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard for visualizing real-time/historical data (crane trajectory, validation metrics) via Grafana"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataVisualizationService
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ValidationMetrics
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service comparing simulated vs. real trajectories and enforcing thresholds (e.g., residual swinging motion tolerance)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessedData
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service dynamically computing optimal trajectories based on kinematic model and operational constraints"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OperationalConstraintsData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorModel
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

instance ArduinoController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Arduino-based microcontrollers managing low-level signal processing for actuator control"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GantryArmActuator
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CartActuator
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PuckCarrierActuator
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time service adjusting crane movements to minimize residual swinging motion during hoisting/traversing operations"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationAction
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service recording operational metrics (motor currents, positional data) over time in TimescaleDB"
    DTDFVocab:provides ManualInterventionAction
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service displaying real-time and historical data through Grafana dashboards for operator monitoring"
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating digital twin accuracy against real-time sensor data, ensuring alignment between physical and digital states"
    DTDFVocab:provides ResidualSwingingInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationThresholdAlert
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service dynamically computing optimal trajectories for container movements, optimizing for efficiency and collision avoidance"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalConstraintViolationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ResidualSwingingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight regarding discrepancies between physical and digital states detected during continuous validation"
]

instance OperationalConstraintViolationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight identifying when operational constraints (payload limits, speed thresholds) are breached"
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action dynamically adjusting trajectories based on real-time sensor feedback"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ValidationThresholdAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action triggering Grafana dashboard alerts when validation metrics exceed predefined thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ManualInterventionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual action allowing operators to override automated trajectory adjustments via HMI"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataCorrectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action applying data corrections to align physical and digital states during continuous validation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance LabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryArmActuator,
        CartActuator,
        PuckCarrierActuator,
        SwingAngleEncoder,
        EnvironmentalSensor,
        KinematicModel,
        TrajectoryGeneratorModel,
        SimulationEnvironment,
        TimescaleDBDataStore,
        RealTimeData,
        ProcessedData,
        OperationalConstraintsData
]

instance PhysicalCraneSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lab-scale gantry crane system comprising physical components (actuators, sensors) interfaced with digital twin via MQTT"
    base:isContainedIn LabEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized digital twin instance orchestrating real-time synchronization between physical crane and simulation models"
    base:isContainedIn LabEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual to Physical Interaction** involves command flows from the digital twin to the physical crane through the **Control Application** (a Python application) and **Trajectory Generator Service**. The **Trajectory Generator Service** generates optimal movement trajectories using a kinematics model, ensuring minimal residual swinging motion.

Commands are executed via the **Physical Crane Interface**, which translates digital commands into hardware-level instructions. Feedback mechanisms are implicitly handled by the **Continuous Validation Service**, which compares executed trajectories against simulations. If discrepancies exceed predefined thresholds, the system notifies operators via the **Dashboard** for manual intervention rather than automatic adjustments. No explicit authentication or validation mechanisms (e.g., message signing, rate limiting) are described, but the system assumes secure communication through the MQTT broker.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** system employs a **low-cost greenhouse** with behavioral adaptation between the physical and digital twin. While the documents do not specify exact synchronization frequencies, it relies on **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage and **ActiveMQ** for message broker communication, which typically supports sub-second latency for real-time adaptation. The virtual machine images include prerecorded data, implying preloaded temporal datasets for offline analysis.

  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses a **Raspberry-Pi** with sensor data processed via **Microsoft Azure tools**, enabling runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction. The temporal granularity is implied by the need for **real-time air quality monitoring** and **virus spread prevention modeling**, though exact synchronization intervals are not detailed. The system likely operates within milliseconds to seconds for immediate feedback loops.

  **Not in Document** for the **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** due to insufficient temporal details provided in the context."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
The GreenhouseDT exemplar implements a **self-adaptive digital twin constellation** with **closed-loop behavioral adaptation** between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. The system relies on:
- **Architecture Pattern**: **Event-driven microservices** (via ActiveMQ) for real-time adaptation.
- **Component Integration**: Virtual machines with pre-recorded datasets for offline twin initialization, supplemented by **InfluxDB** for dynamic time-series synchronization.
- **Resource Management**: Containerized deployment (via VMs) ensures scalability and isolation of twin components.
- **System-Wide Coordination**: Behavioral adaptation is governed by **rule-based algorithms** (e.g., climate control thresholds) triggered by sensor events, with no explicit mention of orchestration frameworks.

The Air Quality Management exemplar defines a **model-driven digital twin constellation** with three applications:
1. **Runtime Visualization**: Edge-based (Raspberry-Pi) for real-time air quality monitoring.
2. **Simulation**: Cloud-based (Azure) for historical/forecasting analysis.
3. **Prediction**: Hybrid (edge-cloud) for virus spread mitigation modeling.
Orchestration is achieved via:
- **Architecture Pattern**: **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** with AutomationML for twin model definition.
- **Component Integration**: **Azure IoT Hub** for event-driven data exchange between edge and cloud.
- **Resource Management**: Containerization (implicit via Raspberry-Pi + cloud deployment) for modular twin components.
- **System-Wide Coordination**: **AutomationML** ensures interoperability between simulation engines and sensor data streams, enabling **cross-twin synchronization** for predictive analytics.

The lab-scale gantry crane system’s **C4 Model** suggests a **centralized twin constellation** with a **single twin instance** managing crane operations. The **context diagram** implies:
- **Architecture Pattern**: **Actor-based control** (e.g., twin as a central orchestrator for actuator commands).
- **Component Integration**: No explicit mention of external frameworks; relies on **C4 Model** for visualization.
- **Resource Management**: Implicit containerization (via VMs or Raspberry-Pi) for twin deployment.
- **System-Wide Coordination**: **No multi-twin coordination** or explicit orchestration framework is described.

"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The provided documents do not explicitly detail formal lifecycle stages for any of the Digital Twin systems (GreenhouseDT, Air Quality Management, or Lab Scale Gantry Crane). Each exemplar appears to operate in an ad-hoc manner without structured lifecycle governance. The GreenhouseDT and Air Quality Management exemplars rely on pre-recorded data in virtual machines and sensor inputs, respectively, but no explicit phases (e.g., deployment, maintenance, obsolescence) or transition management protocols are described. The Gantry Crane system is described using the C4 Model for architecture visualization but lacks explicit lifecycle documentation beyond its domain-specific context (logistics/cyber-physical systems). Thus, no lifecycle stages are explicitly documented in the provided context.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The fidelity and validity of the **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** Digital Twin are ensured through:
- **Continuous Validation Service**, which **compares executed trajectories** (physical vs. simulated) using **distance metrics** to detect deviations.
- **Acceptable threshold-based validation**, though specifics (e.g., '5 cm' threshold) are not provided; the system relies on **generalized error detection** without detailed Monte Carlo simulations or sensor noise quantification.
No explicit mention of motor current-based failure detection or Euclidean distance calculations is present in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** relies on a **centralized, single-node architecture** where all software components (database, broker, dashboard, and services) are deployed on a **personal computer** (not edge-cloud hybrid). Communication is handled via the **MQTT Broker (Eclipse Mosquitto)**, enabling lightweight, low-latency messaging between services.

Network infrastructure details are minimal, but the system prioritizes reliability by leveraging:
- **MQTT protocol** for pub/sub messaging.
- **TimescaleDB** for time-series data storage (no explicit latency/bandwidth specs, but assumed to support real-time operations).

No security protocols like TLS or HTTPS are mentioned; the focus is on the **MQTT broker’s built-in security features** (e.g., authentication via MQTT credentials). The topology is **simplified and localized**, with no redundancy or multi-AZ deployments.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** follows a **single-node, on-premise deployment** model, hosted on a **personal computer**. The infrastructure comprises two distinct deployment layers:

1. **Physical Layer**:
   - The **Physical Crane** hardware is directly connected to the system’s host machine.
   - Python-based services (e.g., **Control Application**, **Trajectory Generator Service**) are deployed as standalone modules (`gantrylib`) installed via `pip`, bundled in a single Python package.

2. **Virtual Layer**:
   - **Containerized components**:
     - **Database**: TimescaleDB, containerized using Docker Compose.
     - **MQTT Broker**: Eclipse Mosquitto, containerized via Docker Compose.
     - **Dashboard**: Grafana, containerized via Docker Compose.
   - **Computational/storage requirements**: Not specified, but the system relies on the host’s native resources for all services.
   - **Scalability**: Not applicable due to single-node architecture; no horizontal scaling or load balancing is implemented.
   - **Deployment models**: Implicitly **monolithic deployment** for Python services and **containerized deployment** for database, broker, and dashboard.
   - **Availability/reliability**: Assumes 100% uptime of the host machine; no redundancy or failover mechanisms are described.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
The GreenhouseDT exemplar integrates **externally** via:
- **Data Exchange Protocols**: **ActiveMQ** for message brokering between physical sensors and the digital twin.
- **Interoperability Standards**: No explicit standards are mentioned; relies on **InfluxDB** for time-series data ingestion.
- **Dependencies**: External systems include **pre-recorded VM datasets** (offline) and **InfluxDB** (cloud-based storage).
- **Integration Patterns**: **Ad-hoc containerization** (via VMs) for twin deployment, with no commercial frameworks.

The Air Quality Management exemplar integrates **externally** through:
- **Data Exchange Protocols**: **AutomationML** for twin model interoperability with construction engineering tools; **Azure IoT Hub** for IoT sensor data ingestion.
- **Interoperability Standards**: **AutomationML** ensures compatibility between edge sensors (Raspberry-Pi) and cloud-based simulations.
- **Dependencies**: External systems include **Microsoft Azure** for cloud analytics, **Raspberry-Pi sensors** for edge data acquisition, and **construction engineering tools** for air quality modeling.
- **Integration Patterns**: **Hybrid edge-cloud architecture** with **event-driven synchronization** (e.g., sensor → cloud twin for predictions).

The lab-scale gantry crane system’s **horizontal integration** is implied via:
- **Data Exchange Protocols**: No explicit protocols mentioned; assumed to use **standard industrial protocols** (e.g., Modbus, CAN) for actuator/sensor communication.
- **Interoperability Standards**: No standards like AutomationML or IoT Hub are referenced; relies on **C4 Model** for architectural visualization.
- **Dependencies**: External systems include **physical actuators** (e.g., motors) and **sensors** (e.g., position encoders) for crane control.
- **Integration Patterns**: **Centralized twin orchestration** with **no mention of external twin systems** or enterprise integration frameworks.

**Note**: The documents do not provide explicit details on **enterprise system integration** (e.g., ERP, SCADA) for any exemplar, thus **horizontal integration with broader enterprise ecosystems is not described**.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
**GreenhouseDT:**
- Uses **commonly used technologies** (InfluxDB, ActiveMQ, virtual machines) but does not explicitly mention industry standards or compliance frameworks.
- Operates in an **ad-hoc approach** without reliance on commercial digital twin frameworks.

**Air Quality Management:**
- Utilizes **AutomationML** for digital twin interoperability and **Microsoft Azure tools** for implementation.
- **No explicit mention of certification requirements, compliance frameworks, or interoperability standards** beyond AutomationML.

**Lab Scale Gantry Crane:**
- Described as using an **ad-hoc approach** without commercial digital twin frameworks, as explicitly stated in the context.
- **No mention of industry standards, certifications, or compliance frameworks** in the provided documents.
"
]
